content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and integers KKK and MMM. For 1≤i≤j≤N1 \le i \le j \le N1≤i≤j≤N, let's define S(i,j)S(i, j)S(i,j) as the number of ways to choose exactly KKK elements of the contiguous subsequence Ai,Ai+1,…,AjA_i, A_{i+1}, \dots, A_jAi​,Ai+1​,…,Aj​ in such a way that the median of these KKK elements is ≥M\ge M≥M.

Find the sum of S(i,j)S(i, j)S(i,j) over all i,ji, ji,j such that 1≤i≤j≤N1 \le i \le j \le N1≤i≤j≤N. Since this sum may be large, calculate it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: All elements of A are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers N, K, and M. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — ∑i=1N∑j=iNS(i, j) modulo 10^9+7.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a dynamic programming approach. We will iterate over all possible subsequences of the given array and calculate the median for each subsequence. If the median is greater than or equal to M, we will increment our count. We will use a modulo operation to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2 * K) — because we are iterating over all possible subsequences of the given array and calculating the median for each subsequence.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we are not using any extra space that scales with the input size.
